title: Question tool input & state.input contracts
description: Data contracts for tool input normalization pipeline. Defines canonical (normalized) shape, accepted raw shapes, and state.input persistence shape.

description.description: Explanation of choice; falls back to label if originally missing

header.description: Short label (max 30 chars); auto-generated from question.slice(0,30) if originally missing

custom.description: Allow free-text answer (default true at tool layer; omitted in tool schema via z.omit)

QuestionToolInputCanonical.description: The canonical / normalized tool input shape that QuestionTool.execute receives after Tool.define wrapper parses args

questions.description: One or more questions to ask

QuestionToolInputRawFlat.description: Accepted noncompliant flat shape (Codex/OpenAI style). Normalized by Question.normalize into canonical form.

options.description: Either string[] or array of {label/value, description/detail/explanation}

QuestionToolInputRawArray.description: Accepted noncompliant multi-question shape where options may still be raw strings.

ToolPartStateInputCompleted.description: state.input field value for completed tool call. For QuestionTool, equals QuestionToolInputCanonical. For tools without preprocess, equals their declared zod schema's canonical shape.
ToolPartStateInputCompleted.$comment: DD-3: processor safeParses value.input using ToolRegistry.get(toolName).parameters and writes parsed result; raw fallback if safeParse fails.

ToolPartStateInputError.description: state.input field value for errored tool call. Kept as LLM raw args for debugging; may be un-normalizable.
ToolPartStateInputError.$comment: DD-3: error status preserves raw for forensic evidence.

inputTypes.description: Accepted inputs: unknown (any); null/primitive passes through as-is; object -> dispatched to flat-wrap or per-item normalize

outputInvariants.description: Canonical inputs round-trip unchanged; flat inputs wrap to {questions:[...]}; string options coerce to {label,description}; missing header fills via question.slice(0,30)

errorHandling.description: Pure data transform; never throws; malformed input yields best-effort output, not an exception

QuestionInfo.header.source: provided by LLM OR fallback from question.slice(0,30)

QuestionOption.label.source: provided by LLM OR if option was string, equal to the string itself

QuestionOption.description.source: provided by LLM OR fallback to label
